body: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) topped the net foreign buy leaderboard on Monday, with a net buy of 85.37 million shares, driving its stock price up 3.2% to IDR 4,220, with a transaction volume of 167.34 million shares worth IDR 699.03 billion. PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (BIPI) followed as the runner-up, with a net buy of 68.23 million shares, closing at IDR 89, up 1.1% from the previous day, with a transaction volume of 246.51 million shares worth IDR 22.11 billion.
title: Bank Rakyat Indonesia and Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur top net foreign buy leaderboard on Monday.

body: PT Griptha Putra Persada Tbk (GRPH) share price plummeted 51.45% to IDR 50 from its initial public offering price of IDR 98 per share on January 22, 2024, with IPO proceeds of IDR 20.6 billion allocated towards hotel facility improvements, new equipment, and working capital. The company's share volume was traded at 175,900 with 18 transactions worth IDR 8.79 million last week.
title: PT Griptha Putra Persada's share price plummets 51.45% to IDR 50 since its initial public offering in January.

body: PT Buyung Poetra Sembada Tbk (HOKI) has signed an agreement to purchase rice products from PT Gurih Mitra Perkasa worth IDR 130 billion, which is less than 20% of its equity of IDR 671.30 billion as of September 2024. The transaction is expected to boost HOKI's rice sales, which already reached IDR 1.05 trillion in 2024, contributing to the company's total sales of IDR 1.06 trillion.
title: PT Buyung Poetra Sembada to purchase rice products from PT Gurih Mitra Perkasa for IDR 130 billion.

body: PT Jasnita Telekomindo Tbk (JAST) has secured a three-year contract to manage contact centre services for the Ministry of Education, Culture, Research, and Technology, contributing to a 30% revenue increase in early 2025 compared to 2024. In September 2024, JAST's revenue reached IDR 103.72 billion, up from IDR 93.98 billion in Q3 2023, with a net profit of IDR 218.66 million, reversing a net loss of IDR 3.2 billion in September 2023.
title: PT Jasnita Telekomindo secures three-year contract to manage contact centre services for Ministry of Education, Culture, Research, and Technology.

body: Bank Indonesia's survey projects credit distribution to grow in Q1 2025, albeit at a slower pace, with a weighted net balance of 82.3%, prioritizing wholesale, retail, and manufacturing sectors. The outstanding credit is expected to increase by 10.34% year-on-year by the end of 2025, with third-party funds projected to decline in Q1 2025, reaching 68.2% compared to 89.3% in the previous quarter.
title: Bank Indonesia Projects Slower but Still Growing Credit Distribution in Q1 2025

body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange has temporarily suspended trading of PT Raharja Energi Cepu TBK (RATU) and PT Raja Roti Cemerlang TBK (BRRC) stocks, including BRRC's Series I warrants, due to significant cumulative price movements, with RATU shares surging 24.71% to IDR5,400 per share after a brief suspension, while BRRC shares have plummeted 71.37% to IDR75 per share since its debut on January 9, 2025. BRRC's Series I warrants have also fallen 78.95% to IDR8, prompting the exchange to place the stocks under surveillance.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Temporarily Suspends Trading of Two Stocks Due to Significant Price Movements.

body: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara Tbk (TOWR) is acquiring a 40% stake in PT Remala Abadi Tbk through its subsidiary PT Iforte Solusi Infotek, with the goal of expanding its business network and strengthening its position in the telecommunications digital infrastructure sector. The acquisition, valued at an undisclosed amount, will make Djarum Group, through Iforte, the controlling shareholder of Data, which operates internet services, networking, and IT solutions businesses through fiber optic and wireless technologies.
title: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara to Acquire 40% Stake in PT Remala Abadi through Subsidiary.

body: PT Bank Tabungan Negara Tbk (BBTN) plans to acquire PT Bank Victoria Syariah's sharia business unit for IDR 1.06 trillion, to be funded using internal funds, as part of its corporate strategic plan to separate its sharia business unit. The acquisition, expected to be completed in May 2025, has received approval from the Financial Services Authority and will require shareholder approval on March 14, 2025.
title: PT Bank Tabungan Negara to Acquire PT Bank Victoria Syariah's Sharia Business Unit for IDR 1.06 Trillion

body: PT Humpuss Maritim Internasional Tbk (HUMI) has allocated a capital expenditure budget of USD 39.57 million for 2025 to strengthen its fleet and improve energy efficiency across key business segments, with plans to acquire 10 new vessels, including oil and chemical tankers, tugboats, and a platform supply vessel. The company aims to improve profitability margins and support global sustainability agendas through its strategic approach, which includes implementing a maintenance system and optimizing bunker fuel efficiency.
title: Humpuss Maritim Allocates $39.57 Million to Strengthen Fleet and Improve Energy Efficiency in 2025.

body: Performance food-related stocks, such as PT Charoen Pokphand Indonesia Tbk (CPIN) and PT Japfa Comfeed Indonesia Tbk (JPFA), are expected to maintain stable profitability in 2H 2024 despite concerns over declining broiler chicken prices, with JPFA ranked first and CPIN second in terms of performance. Looking ahead to 2025, both companies are projected to maintain profitability, driven by the Indonesian government's food estate and free nutritious meals programs, although they still face challenges related to self-culling practices.
title: Food-related stocks in Indonesia expected to remain stable despite anticipated weaker results in second half of 2024.

body: PT Bayan Resources Tbk (BYAN) has appointed Hendarman, S.H. as its new President Commissioner, effective until the 2027 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders, replacing Purnomo Yusgiantoro who resigned to become a special advisor to the Merah Putih Cabinet. Following the announcement, BYAN's share price increased by 0.12% or 25 points to IDR 20,500 per share, with a 3.40% or 675-point rise over the past year.
title: PT Bayan Resources appoints new President Commissioner Hendarman through Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders.

body: PT Akr Indonesia Tbk (AKRA) has dissolved its business unit, PT Energi Manyar Sejahtera (EMS), which was established in 2015 and owned 45% of its shares through its subsidiary PT Usaha Era Pratama Nusantara. The transaction is not considered material, and other shareholders of EMS include PT Sanitiluwansa Lestari, PT Amanah Indo Invest, and PT Berlian Jasa Terminal Indonesia, a entity under Pelindo Group.
title: PT Akr Indonesia dissolves power generation services unit PT Energi Manyar Sejahtera.

body: Foreign investors in Indonesia's stock market recorded a net buy of 603.78 million shares on Friday, January 17, with PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) being the top buy with 2.68 billion shares purchased. Meanwhile, PT MNC Land Tbk (MNC) led the top sell list with a net sale of 81.09 million shares, followed by PT Central Proteina Prima Tbk (CPRO) with a net sale of almost 50 million shares.
title: Foreigners Net Buy 603.78 Million Shares in Indonesian Market on Friday

body: Newport Marine (BOAT) reported a 20.2% year-over-year increase in sales to USD 8.1 million in September 2024, driven by the recovery of global oil commodity prices and higher charter rates. The company anticipates improved cash flow and future expansion, with USD 1 million allocated for capital expenditures in 2025 to update its fleet, and is optimistic about maintaining its positive growth trend in 2025.
title: Newport Marine Reports 20.2% Year-on-Year Sales Growth in September 2024
